title: Arrays, Pointers, Arithmetic, and Bounds · Memory Retrieval
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the bounded image-slice library contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded image-slice library after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve array extent where possible and predict array-to-pointer conversion at expressions and parameters.
Perform pointer arithmetic only within one array object or one-past it and avoid dereferencing one-past pointers.
State and check lower and upper index invariants before access, including zero-length and changed-shape cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
